rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL LIND ET AL VS. AERON NOWELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award And For Entry Of Judgment Thereon
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 18, 2021 line 1. PETITIONER MICHAEL LIND, KARA LIND Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award And For Entry Of Judgment Thereon is OFF CALENDAR. Re-noticed for April 22, 2021. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND VINCENT DIGIACOMO JR. VS. TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to ANSWER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 18, 2021 line 2. PLAINTIFF RAYMOND DIGIACOMO DEMURRER to ANSWER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. Affirmative defenses are sufficiently alleged. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

DEFENDANT 367 LIBERTY STREET, LLC ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action Pending Arbitration is GRANTED in part. rulings.Case Title: GABRIELLE FEUERSINGER VS. LOOLY'S PBC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 18, 2021 line 6. DEFENDANT LOOLY'S PBC DBA WETHETRILLIONS DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, assuming that Defendant is in good standing by April 1, 2021, Defendant to file and serve answer no later than 9:00 a.m. on April 1, 2021. Defendant fails to cite any authority for the proposition that allegations regarding applicability/non-applicability of SF Admin. Code 37.C are elements of unlawful detainer cause of action. If defendant is not in good standing as of 9:00 a.m. on April 1, 2021, the demurrer is taken off calendar and Plaintiff may apply for entry of default on April 1, 2021 after 9:30 a.m. Clerk of the Court to check for Answer at 9:30a.m. on April 1, 2021 and take the demurrer off calendar should there be no Answer submitted for filing by 9:00 a.m. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
